nodes.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Guard link attribute parsing against non-dict values**

When a span-start event includes a `links` entry whose `attributes` field is not a mapping (for example a string or list), `_build_links` calls `.items()` unconditionally and raises `AttributeError`, which aborts `emit()` for that span. This is a runtime regression introduced with link support, and it contradicts the intended behavior of skipping malformed link entries; the sink should validate `attributes` is a dict (or fall back to `{}`) before iterating.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Good catch. Fixed in 998fe47 — `_build_links` now coerces a non-dict `attributes` field to `{}` before iterating, and a regression test (`test_link_with_non_dict_attributes_does_not_crash`) covers it.

Malformed `links` values are not fully guarded here: if `links` is present but not iterable (for example `True` or `1`), this loop raises `TypeError` and the span start fails instead of skipping the malformed input as the new API contract describes. Validate that `raw_links` is a list/tuple (or other intended iterable) before iterating.
Fixed in 20bf459. `_build_links` now returns early when `links` is truthy but not a list/tuple, so malformed scalar values are skipped instead of raising during span start.

This still crashes for malformed link attributes such as `{"span_id": "source", "attributes": "bad"}` because non-empty non-dict values do not have `.items()`. Since the new behavior promises malformed link entries are skipped without crashing, guard `attributes` with an `isinstance(..., dict)` check before building `link_attrs`.
Fixed in 20bf459. Non-dict `attributes` values are now coerced to `{}` before iteration, and the regression test coverage remains in `test_link_with_non_dict_attributes_does_not_crash`.

The referenced line number is stale: `self._spans` is at `sdk/agentguard/sinks/otel.py:80` in this diff, not `otel.py:70`. Keeping evidence artifacts accurate matters because future reviewers may use these citations to verify the implementation.

This issue also appears on line 12 of the same file.
Updated in 20bf459. The evidence note now points at the current `self._spans` location in `otel.py`.

This malformed-input test only covers bad list entries and missing `span_id`; it does not cover a non-list `links` value or a link whose `attributes` value is non-dict. Those cases are part of the new malformed-input contract and should be covered to prevent regressions in the link parsing path.
Addressed in 20bf459. Added `test_non_iterable_links_value_skipped` alongside the existing malformed-attributes coverage so both malformed-link cases are pinned.

This PASS statement is too broad: the implementation only handles non-dict entries, missing `span_id`, unknown span IDs, and falsey attributes. Non-list `links` values and non-dict truthy `attributes` values still raise exceptions, so the QA evidence should not claim malformed input is fully defended until those cases are fixed/tested.

This issue also appears on line 26 of the same file.
Updated in 20bf459. QA now states the narrower, accurate malformed-input contract: non-list `links`, non-dict entries, missing span IDs, unknown span IDs, and non-dict attrs are skipped safely.

This completion checkbox is not satisfied yet: malformed link inputs are only partially handled, and cases like a non-iterable `links` value or non-dict `attributes` value can still raise during span start. Leave this unchecked or broaden the validation/tests before marking it complete.
Updated in 20bf459. The work-plan completion note now explicitly includes truthy non-list `links` values and non-dict link attributes.
